channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: What Nigerians can use US B1/B2 visa for – Embassy

item.description:

Kano State Government provides N1 million and food items to the family of Inspector Sani Haladu, a police officer who died in the line of duty. This Kano p
Read More: https://punchng.com/kano-donates-food-items-n1m-to-family-of-slain-police-officer/

item.description:
National Security Adviser Nuhu Ribadu meets with US Assistant Secretary Frank Garcia to deepen Nigeria US security cooperation and review progress.

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A 50-year-old Nigerian woman shares her incredible journey of giving birth to quadruplets despite receiving conflicting scan results from doctors.

item.title: 'A little lost' - Why has Mainoo become England's forgotten man?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Kobbie Mainoo has cut a pretty forlorn-looking figure at this World Cup.

item.title: Tinubu-Shettima Ticket: Politics of Loyalty over Calculation
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Iyobosa Uwugiaren argues that President Bola Tinubu’s decision to retain Vice President Kashim Shettima as his running mate ahead of the 2027 general election reinforces an image he has carefully

item.title: Mary Habila: NWIA seeks independent probe into death of Umahi’s physiotherapist
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Nigerian Women International Alliance (NWIA) has called on President Bola Tinubu to ensure an independent, transparent, and credible investigation into the death of physiotherapist Mary Habila, whose reported death at the residence of the Minister of Works has sparked public concern.

item.title: Nigeria’s Untapped Solutions: Between Extraordinary Influence Of Faith And Govt
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: By Carmen Uwaila Ibie For decades, Nigeria has battled multiple crises simultaneously—terrorism, banditry, kidnapping, communal conflict, youth unemployment, poverty, declining public confidence, and economic instability. Governments have changed, security strategies

item.title: Air Force, drones failed to penetrate forest canopy in Oyo rescue, says Army commander
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian Army commander reveals Air Force and drones failed to penetrate the thick Oyo forest canopy during the rescue of abducted pupils and teachers.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: "We do not present those allegations as established facts. Rather, we believe that where multiple concerns arise regarding..." The post Mary Habila: Tinubu urged to investigate death of Ebonyi physiotherapist appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: IMF July economic outlook: Oil lifts Nigeria, AI lifts the World’s winners
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The IMF's July 2026 World Economic Outlook Update projects global growth of 3.0% in 2026 and 3.4% in 2027; slightly below the 3.5% average of 2024–25, though broadly unchanged from April. The post IMF July economic outlook: Oil lifts Nigeria, AI lifts the World’s winners appeared first on Nair

item.title: Final Flight With 315 Nigerians From South Africa To Arrive Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The government had earlier conducted four evacuation flights, bringing home hundreds of Nigerians following the recent xenophobic attacks and security concerns in parts of South Africa. The post Final Flight With 315 Nigerians From South Africa To Arrive Wednesday appeared first on Channels Televis

item.title: Retired Professor bags another Ph.D. at 89
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Beyond science, Nwagwu has carved a distinguished reputation in literature, publishing several volumes of poetry, essays and memoirs. He remains the only Nigerian scholar who is both a Fellow of the Nigerian Academy of Science and a Fellow of the Association of Nigerian Authors.

item.title: FG Recommits to Safer, Modern Aviation Sector
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has reiterated its commitment to transforming Nigeria’s aviation industry through improved safety, modern infrastructure, enhanced passenger experience, job creation and sustained engagement with industry stakeholders. According to a statement by the Director, Press and

item.title: Nigeria contradicts US claim on huge electronic materials ‘captured’ from terrorists
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “From that (Nigerian) raid, we needed an extra plane to bring home all the electronic material that we captured in those camps," a US official said. The post Nigeria contradicts US claim on huge electronic materials ‘captured’ from terrorists appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "I don't want to add extra pressure to (Spain coach) Luis (de la Fuente) and their team -- he knows very well that people are expecting great things of Spain. The post Spain ‘favourites’ says France coach ahead of World Cup semi-final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: AI will require $5 trillion in annual investment by 2040 – SoftBank CEO
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son has said artificial intelligence will require annual investment of $5 trillion by 2040, dismissing growing concerns about an AI bubble as "absurd". The post AI will require $5 trillion in annual investment by 2040 – SoftBank CEO appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.description: Stakeholders in Bayelsa State have endorsed the infrastructure development agenda of Governor Douye Diri’s administration. The post Stakeholders endorse Diri’s infrastructural devt in Bayelsa appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Rivers: Troops recover assault rifle, ammunition in Obiakpor
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Operation UDO KA have reportedly recovered an assault rifle and ammunition during a targeted raid on a suspected cultists’ hideout in Obiakpor Local Government Area of Rivers State. Security expert, Zagazola Makama disclosed this in a report he posted on his official X handle on Tuesd

item.title: Court rejects Ganduje’s request to compel PREMIUM TIMES reporter to disclose sources on port ownership report
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Justice Simon Amobeda of the Federal High Court in Kano ruled that while the court possesses the authority to subpoena a journalist to testify, the reporter retains the right to protect their sources unless those sources are directly linked to the case. The post Court rejects Ganduje’s request

item.title: Lagos govt attributes flooding to rising lagoon water levels
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Lagos State Government has attributed the flooding being experienced in some parts of the state to rising lagoon water levels, saying the elevated water level is preventing stormwater from draining effectively. The post Lagos govt attributes flooding to rising lagoon water levels appeared first

item.title: World Cup 2026: France aim for third consecutive final against Spain
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The French team are taking on Spain in Dallas in the World Cup semi-final. Les Bleus are hoping to become the second nation to reach three consecutive World Cup finals.

item.title: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

item.title: NEPC targets stronger export base as stakeholders push for cashew, sesame, soya bean in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) has intensified efforts to boost Kwara State’s contribution to Nigeria’s non-oil export earnings with a renewed drive to develop export clusters around cashew, sesame seeds and soya beans. The initiative took centre stage at a stakeholders’ consultative m

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A housing expert has lamented the astronomical rent increase in Lagos State, saying that in the next five years, an average Lagosian may find it difficult to pay house rent within the urban areas. To salvage the situation, the Principal Partner of vM. I. Okoro and Associates, Chief Meckson Okoro, ca

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: FIFA has confirmed that Ismail Elfath will be the referee in charge of the 2026 World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina. The American official has already refereed three games at the tournament. Elfath most recently took charge of Norway’s World Cup last 16 win over Brazil. In that

item.title: Trillion Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: At a time of acute investor unease over a selloff in AI-inflated tech stocks, this week’s results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and ASML Holding NV carry even greater significance than usual.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The president of the Senegalese Football Federation said on Monday that the national team's World Cup campaign was overshadowed by internal tensions after head coach Pape Thiaw demanded a salary increase. item.description: Akindele spoke about the sacrifices she made, noting that she gave up social activities and neglected her appearance.

item.description: The City Boy Movement (CBM) had commenced its maiden three-day National Retreat in Abuja, where it inaugurated members of its National Working Committee (NWC) and unveiled an ambitious plan to mobilise 10 million votes in support of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. item.title: Top 5 Stories Of The Day | Freed Oyo School Principal Recounts Ordeal in Terrorists’ Captivity
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Across Nigeria’s 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory, these are the top five Nigerian news stories you shouldn’t miss. Freed Oyo school principal recounts ordeal in terrorists’ captivity Rachael Alamu, principal of Community High School, Esin Ele, has described how terrorists beat some

item.title: PFIPC: ICPC Begins Probe of ‘Fake Agency’, N1.3 billion Budget
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Nigeria’s anti-graft agency has launched an investigation into an alleged fictitious government body and the unauthorized insertion of 1.3 billion naira ($812,000) into the national budget, as the official at the center of the scandal requested an independent panel, citing threats to his life. The I

item.title: Africa: All of Africa Today - July 14, 2026
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica]

item.title: Nigeria: 6,009 Called to the Nigerian Bar, Body of Benchers Cautions Against Misuse of 'Barrister'
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The chairperson of the Body of Benchers, Albert Akpomudje, cautioned against the improper use of 'Barrister' as a prefix before names, noting that it is not a professional title to be used in that manner.

item.title: Nigeria: One Year After Death, Buhari's Life Still 'Speaks' to Nigerians - Tinubu
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] President Tinubu says Mr Buhari's transformation from military ruler to civilian democrat marked him out as a rare figure in the nation's history.

item.title: With US-Iran trust broken again, can Pakistan bring them back to talks?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Islamabad is still urging dialogue, but analysts say it has few tools to halt the latest round of US-Iran escalation.

item.title: Saudi Arabia opens door to LGBTQ visitors ahead of 2034 World Cup
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Under the direction of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Saudi Arabia's de facto ruler, the power of its influential clerics has diminished and the once-feared morality police have been muzzled. The post Saudi Arabia opens door to LGBTQ visitors ahead of 2034 World Cup appeared first on Vanguard New

item.title: Attacks On Commercial Vessels Threaten Global Supply Chains – IMO
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The International Maritime Organisation (IMO) has warned that continued attacks on commercial vessels in conflict zones pose a serious threat to global supply chains, maritime safety and the security of seafarers. IMO Secretary-General, Arsenio Dominguez, made the remark in a statement issued as the

item.title: Malawi: Hundreds of People Still Stranded Outside Malawian Consulate
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [GroundUp] A psychologist working with the MSF team identified people requiring mental health support due to trauma

item.title: We’ll support whatever credible solution Tinubu chooses to end insecurity —Ogene
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The spokesperson for the opposition parties in the House of Representatives and Caucus Leader of Nigerian Democratic Congress (NDC), Honourable Victor Afam Ogene, in this interview with KEHINDE AKINTOLA, gave insight into the hard stance of the opposition parties in the green chamber on the state of

item.title: Atiku, Obi hail WAEC, NECO fee hike suspension
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The suspension is welcome, but it also raises an uncomfortable question: why must this government always wait for public outrage before correcting policies that should never have been conceived in the first place?” Atiku asked.

item.title: Regulators, Investors Push Green Finance To Unlock Africa’s Transition At FITC Summit
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Regulators and investors have urged urgent expansion of green finance instruments — from green bonds to sustainability-linked loans — as the key to unlocking Africa’s low‑carbon transition, closing the continent’s climate finance gap and attracting global capital. The urgent need for Africa to trans

item.title: Zadok Yohanna breaks into 2026 Golden Boy top 100 rankings
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The latest Golden Boy rankings further underline Yohanna's growing reputation, with the Nigerian youngster ranked 80th overall, placing him ahead of his Brighton teammate Babis Kostoulas, who occupies 83rd position. The post Zadok Yohanna breaks into 2026 Golden Boy top 100 rankings appeared first o

item.title: Niger Inaugurates Plant Nursery, Targets Two Million Trees
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Niger government has inaugurated a nursery to drive its plan to plant over two million trees across the state. The plant nursery was inaugurated at Abdulkadir Kure University in Minna by Alhaji Abubakar Musa, the state Commissioner for Environment and Climate Change. Musa said that the initiativ

item.title: Court sentences notorious bandit’s ally to 20 years imprisonment
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Justice Nwite ordered that the sentences must run concurrently, beginning from Jan. 11, 2023, the date of his arrest. The post Court sentences notorious bandit’s ally to 20 years imprisonment appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Gunmen abandoned abducted Oyo farmer, fled – Police
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command said intense security operations forced gunmen to abandon a farmer they had kidnapped along the Igbojaye–Budo Aare bush road, leading to his safe rescue on Monday. The post Gunmen abandoned abducted Oyo farmer, fled – Police appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.
